value.body:
Found during a static review of the desktop renderer issue-list path.

Problem:
The workspace-level issue list fetches one paginated page from each repository independently, merges those page slices, and only then sorts the merged entries. This does not produce a globally ordered page across repositories. If one repository has more matching issues than the page limit, newer/higher-priority issues from its later pages can be hidden behind older issues from another repository's first page.

Evidence:
- `packages/desktop/src/renderer/queries/issues.ts:69-90` routes global issue loading through `listIssuesForRepositories` and sends all repository ids in one request.
- `packages/desktop/src/renderer/queries/issues.ts:93-100` applies a default page limit of 100 per request.
- `packages/desktop/src/renderer/lib/cycleApiClient.ts:494-518` fans out to `listIssuesForRepository` once per repository using independent cursors.
- `packages/desktop/src/renderer/lib/cycleApiClient.ts:526-530` sorts only the already-fetched per-repository page entries.
- `packages/desktop/src/renderer/lib/cycleApiClient.ts:519-524` encodes next cursors only after the first per-repository page has already been chosen.

Suggested remediation:
- Move multi-repository issue listing to a backend query that can sort/page across repositories in one ordered result set, or implement a k-way merge that over-fetches enough candidates to produce a true global page.
- Ensure the returned `nextCursor` preserves the global ordering boundary, not only per-repository cursors.

Acceptance criteria:
- Workspace issue list pages are globally ordered for `updatedAt`, `createdAt`, `dueDate`, `priority`, and `title` sorts across multiple repositories.
- A repository with more than one page of newer issues cannot have page-2 results appear after older page-1 results from another repository.
- Tests cover at least two repositories with uneven issue counts and interleaved sort values.

frontmatter.title: Fix global issue list ordering across repository pages

value.title: Fix global issue list ordering across repository pages
